user_prompt: Generate cookie consent banner copy that complies with both GDPR and ePrivacy.
actual_output:
**GDPR & ePrivacy Compliant Cookie Consent Banner Template**

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance, not legal advice. Cookie consent mechanisms are highly implementation-specific and must be reviewed by qualified legal counsel or your Data Protection Officer. This template reflects key requirements under GDPR Articles 4(11), 6(1)(a), 7, 12–14 and ePrivacy Directive 2002/58/EC Article 5(3), as interpreted by EDPB Guidelines 05/2020 and CJEU case law (e.g. Planet49). It does not constitute binding advice and must be adapted to your specific data processing purposes, cookie taxonomy, and risk profile.*

### Recommended Banner Copy (Layer 1 – Initial Banner)

**Heading:**  
We value your privacy

**Body text:**  
This website uses cookies and similar technologies. Essential cookies are required for the site to function and are always active. Other cookies help us analyse traffic, improve your experience, and deliver personalised content and advertising.

You can accept all cookies, reject non-essential cookies, or customise your preferences at any time. Your choices will be stored for 12 months and can be withdrawn at any time with future effect.

[Learn more in our Cookie Policy and Privacy Policy]

**Buttons (must be equally prominent, no pre-ticked boxes):**
- **Customise preferences** (primary action)
- **Reject all** 
- **Accept all**

### Customisation / Preferences Layer (Layer 2 – Modal or Second Screen)

**Heading:**  
Manage cookie preferences

**Introductory text:**  
We use different types of cookies. You can review and change your choices below. For detailed information on each cookie, including duration and providers, please see our full [Cookie Policy].

**Granular toggles (with strict necessity exempt from consent):**

**Strictly necessary**  
These cookies are essential for the website to function and cannot be switched off. They are set in response to your actions such as logging in or filling forms.  
**Always active** (non-toggleable, greyed out)

**Performance / Analytics**  
These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us understand which pages are most popular and how visitors move around the site.  
[On/Off toggle]  
*Lawful basis: Consent (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a))*

**Functional / Preference**  
These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third-party providers whose services we have added to our pages.  
[On/Off toggle]

**Marketing / Advertising**  
These cookies may be used to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant advertising on other sites. They do not store personal data directly but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and device.  
[On/Off toggle]

**Social media / Third-party embedding**  
These cookies are set by social media services and other third parties to enable you to share content and interact with their platforms.  
[On/Off toggle]

**Footer controls (always visible):**  
- **Save my preferences** (must record granular consent with timestamp, IP hash or unique identifier, and version of the policy)
- **Reject all**
- **Accept all**

**Link to full Cookie Policy** (must contain at minimum: cookie categories, specific cookies with purposes, providers, expiry periods, and transfer information where applicable).

### Key Compliance Notes (Remediation Checklist)

- **Prior consent (ePrivacy Art. 5(3))**: Non-essential cookies and trackers must not be set until the user has actively clicked “Accept all”, “Save my preferences”, or individually enabled a category. Strictly necessary cookies (Art. 5(3) exception) may be set immediately.
- **Freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous consent (GDPR Art. 4(11) & Art. 7)**: No bundling of purposes. No “consent by continued browsing”. Reject option must be as easy as accept. No dark patterns (e.g. pre-selected toggles, obscured reject button, or making the site unusable if consent is withheld).
- **Transparency (GDPR Art. 12–14)**: Clear and plain language. Link to detailed cookie policy that lists each cookie, purpose, duration, and any third-country transfers with appropriate safeguards (e.g. SCCs or adequacy decision).
- **Withdraw consent (GDPR Art. 7(3))**: Provide a persistent “Cookie settings” link in the footer that allows users to change or withdraw consent at any time. Record and honour withdrawal promptly.
- **Record-keeping (GDPR Art. 7(1) & Art. 30)**: Log consent events (who, when, what was consented to, version of banner text, method of consent). Retain for audit purposes.
- **Data processors & third parties (GDPR Art. 28)**: Ensure all third-party cookies have appropriate Data Processing
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and compliant cookie banner template, cited relevant GDPR articles, and included necessary legal disclaimers as required by its system prompt.
